assertions.expectation: Addresses Istio control plane version compatibility with the target Kubernetes version
assertions.reason: Response checks current Istio version and verifies GKE 1.30 compatibility, stating 'Istio 1.20+ generally supports GKE 1.30'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the Istio-Kubernetes version compatibility matrix before upgrading GKE
assertions.reason: Response includes pre-upgrade assessment with commands to verify GKE 1.30 compatibility with current Istio version

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading Istio first if the current Istio version doesn't support the target Kubernetes version
assertions.reason: Response includes a pro tip with specific command to upgrade Istio first if version is too old for GKE 1.30

assertions.expectation: Explains that sidecar injection happens via mutating webhook and may cause issues during node drain/recreation
assertions.reason: Response mentions sidecar injection issues in red flags but doesn't explain the mutating webhook mechanism or node drain/recreation specifics

assertions.expectation: Mentions that new pods on upgraded nodes will get sidecar injected — verify the sidecar version matches
assertions.reason: Response doesn't specifically mention that new pods on upgraded nodes will get sidecar injected or the need to verify sidecar version matching

assertions.expectation: Recommends monitoring Istio proxy version consistency across the mesh after upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes monitoring commands for proxy health and mentions checking for proxy version consistency in the monitoring section

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check Istio version and proxy sync status (istioctl or kubectl)
assertions.reason: Response includes 'istioctl version', 'istioctl proxy-status', and kubectl commands to check proxy sync status

assertions.expectation: Warns about potential traffic disruption if Istio control plane and sidecar versions are mismatched
assertions.reason: Response warns about version mismatches in red flags section mentioning 503 errors and services becoming unreachable, which implies traffic disruption from version mismatches
